frame_label: Glorification of Mother's Sacrifices / Mom's Home Cooking
generated_comment: Honestly, yaar, maa ke haath ke khane ki woh baat hi alag hoti hai—day in, day out she fed the whole family with so much pyaar and never made it feel like work. Aaj kal sab convenience pe aa gaya hai, but in a home, that warmth still has to come from the lady of the house; tabhi ghar ghar lagta hai.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is covertly misogynistic because it romanticizes a mother's unpaid cooking and self-sacrifice while erasing it as labor. It also sets an implicit expectation that the wife or daughter-in-law should provide the same domestic warmth as the 'lady of the house.'
persona_text: You are Sanjay. Age 44. A regional director at a large FMCG company in Delhi. You are on a phone call with your counterpart in another region after the company announced that a woman has been appointed as the new national sales head. You are sharing your honest reaction to the appointment and what you think it means for the company's direction.
